name: Saw by the Era del Violín – circular (SL-A 13)
name_original: Sierro por la Era del Violín – circular (SL-A 13)
region_intro: Hiking around Sierro offers access to a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, valleys, and historical mining areas within the Almería region. The terrain often features a mix of arid and semi-arid environments, with occasional green pockets along watercourses or in higher elevations. Hikers can expect paths that traverse agricultural lands, natural rock formations, and areas with historical significance. The region provides a variety of trails suitable for different experience levels. Best hiking trails around Sierro The most popular hiking route is Saw by the Era del Violín – circular (SL-A 13), a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This circular path explores the local natural features and offers varied scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tahal Circular Trail, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This route provides an immersive experience through the local landscape, often featuring open views and natural surroundings. Local hikers also love the Las Minas de Talco – circular between Lucar and Somontin (PR-A 301), an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through historical mining landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Hiking around Sierro is defined by its varied terrain, including hills, valleys, and historical sites like old mines. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Sierro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 130 hikers have used komoot to explore Sierro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sierro?
A: Sierro offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find routes ranging from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Sierro?
A: Yes, Sierro has several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14600272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de las Focas of Macael – Monument to the Mother loop from Macael&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.3-mile (5.2 km) route that offers a pleasant experience without significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Sierro?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Sierro are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983297317/saw-by-the-era-del-violin-circular-sl-a-13" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saw by the Era del Violín – circular (SL-A 13)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 3.9-mile (6.3 km) path that showcases varied scenery.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Sierro?
A: Hikes in Sierro vary significantly. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38273973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Menas to Bacares – Barrancón River Waterfall loop from Bacares&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 3 miles (4.8 km) and takes around 1 hour 20 minutes. Longer trails, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984231133/las-minas-de-talco-circular-between-lucar-and-somontin-pr-a-301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Minas de Talco – circular between Lucar and Somontin (PR-A 301)&lt;/a&gt;, can be up to 8.3 miles (13.4 km) and take nearly 4 hours.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural monuments to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts several natural attractions. You can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2650515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Huevo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Estrecho de Urrácal&lt;/a&gt; gorge. These natural monuments add a unique element to your hiking experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sierro?
A: The best time to hike in Sierro is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be cooler at higher elevations.

Q: Can I hike with my dog on trails near Sierro?
A: Many trails in the Sierro area are suitable for dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet, as water sources can be scarce in this arid region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Sierro?
A: The terrain around Sierro is quite varied, featuring rolling hills, valleys, and historical mining areas. You'll encounter paths traversing agricultural lands, natural rock formations, and sometimes semi-arid environments, with occasional green pockets along watercourses.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore while hiking?
A: Sierro's history, particularly its mining heritage, is evident in the landscape. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984231133/las-minas-de-talco-circular-between-lucar-and-somontin-pr-a-301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Minas de Talco – circular between Lucar and Somontin (PR-A 301)&lt;/a&gt; lead through old mining areas. You might also encounter historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4807759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serón Railway Station&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5306706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Tíjola train Station&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Sierro?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the sense of tranquility, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites like the old mining landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Sierro?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For most popular routes, you can usually find designated parking areas in nearby villages or at the start of the trail. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for information on parking facilities before you set out.

Q: Are there any challenging or difficult hikes in Sierro?
A: While many trails are moderate, Sierro does offer some more challenging options for experienced hikers. These routes often involve greater elevation changes and longer distances, providing a more strenuous workout and rewarding views. There are 3 difficult routes available for those seeking a challenge.

Medieval castle-fortress of Islamic Berber style. With a difference of 300 m above the river that crosses the town of Sierro, this castle stands out for its prominence and good views of the entire territory that surrounds it. It dates from the 12th century and its reconstruction is from the year 2011.
